reasoning: SPGI has experienced recent negative momentum, dropping from around $520 to roughly $506 in the past week despite string Q1 earnings from late April. The statistical trend model points to a continued near-term probability of less than 0.5 for a price increase (combined tool estimate suggests 0.41 to 0.48 by June 1). In the short term (4 trading days until June 1), the immediate downward momentum weighs heavily. Moving out onto longer horizons, short-term momentum washes out and the general positive risk premium of the broader equity market slowly reasserts itself, pushing expectations closer and closer to a coin toss, then slightly favoring a price increase over a 6-month timeframe. Hence, probabilities step from 0.45 (1 week) towards 0.51 (6 months), honoring the increasing uncertainty and mean-reverting qualities of long-term stock prices.
